vitals.aid_stations: Living bases with rest, food, sleeping areas
vitals.time_limit: Details coming soon; structured by distance (e.g., 1000 km finishes by March 29)
vitals.participation_trend: New 2026 event in Race Across Series

course_description.overview: Ultra-distance self-supported road cycling race from Atlantic coast through Portugal's mountainous interior, with distances of 1000 km, 500 km, 300 km, and 200 km options. Mild climate, varied landscapes from rugged mountains to picturesque villages and coastlines.[1]

key_segments.name: 1000 km Solo
key_segments.description: Full ultra-distance challenge requiring prior ultra experience; departure from March 25 evening.[1]

key_segments.name: 500 km
key_segments.description: Mid-distance option starting March 27; bib collection and briefing March 26.[1]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Self-supported ultra-cycling with strict entry qualifications for longer distances (e.g., prior Race Across finisher or equivalent experience). Mountainous terrain and ~11,100 m elevation for 1000 km demand high endurance.[1][7]

logistics.getting_there: Porto and surrounding area; fly into Porto Airport (OPO) for northern Portugal start near Vila Meã.[1][8]
logistics.accommodation: Living bases provide sleeping areas; pre-race meals and post-race BBQ/party.[1]
logistics.registration_details: Entry requirements for 1000 km: finisher of prior Race Across events or equivalent; registration opens October 2025, closes March 2026.[1][7][8]
logistics.bike_logistics: Self-supported; operational management station (PGO) for tracking and support.[1]
logistics.local_considerations: Mild early spring climate ideal for ultras; awards March 29 at 10 a.m.[1]

final_verdict.one_liner: Challenging self-supported ultra-road race kicking off the 2026 season in Portugal's diverse landscapes.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced ultra-cyclists seeking a qualifier-friendly event with mountainous terrain and mild weather.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those qualified and wanting a strong season opener with solid organization.
pro_tips: Verify qualifications early for 1000 km entry.
Train for ~11,000 m elevation over 1000 km.
Leverage living bases for strategic rests.
Book Porto flights soon for northern start.
